summary: PR 219 reorganized a large portion of Dolphin GX assembly by splitting code out of asm/dolphin/gx/GXFifo.s into new GX-related assembly files and wiring those new objects into the build. It also replaced many address-based func_8033xxxx names with known GX SDK-style symbol names in GXFifo, GXInit, include/functions.h, and downstream call sites. There was no PR body or review discussion in the slice, so the rationale is inferred from the title and diff structure: make the GX module less monolithic and improve symbol naming for future decompilation.
what_changed: Added asm/dolphin/gx/GX.s with 6473 lines of GX assembly, including __GXSetDirtyState and many following GX routines still partly using func_8033xxxx labels.
Added asm/dolphin/gx/GXAttr.s with 1194 lines, separating vertex attribute-related GX code from the previous layout.

Added asm/dolphin/gx/GXMisc.s with 365 lines, separating miscellaneous GX/PE-style routines such as GXSetMisc, GXSetDrawDone, GXWaitDrawDone, GXPoke* names, and __GXPEInit as reflected in include/functions.h.
Reduced asm/dolphin/gx/GXFifo.s by roughly 8054 deleted lines while keeping FIFO-specific code there.
Added GXAttr.o, GXMisc.o, and GX.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk immediately after GXInit.o and GXFifo.o.

Renamed FIFO functions from anonymous address labels to known GX names, including GXInitFifoBase, GXInitFifoPtrs, GXInitFifoLimits, GXSetCPUFifo, GXSetGPFifo, __GXFifoInit, __GXFifoReadEnable, __GXFifoReadDisable, __GXFifoLink, __GXWriteFifoIntEnable, and __GXWriteFifoIntReset.
Updated GXInit.s call sites from func_8033BC34, func_8033B8C4, and func_8033B9AC to __GXFifoInit, GXInitFifoBase, and GXSetCPUFifo.
Updated include/functions.h comments/prototypes to use the newly identified GX symbol names instead of func_8033xxxx placeholders.
Touched many melee and sysdolphin assembly files with small one-for-one edits, likely to update branch/call symbol references after the GX split and renaming.
smart_moves: The split preserved a build-visible object order by adding GXAttr.o, GXMisc.o, and GX.o directly after existing GXInit.o and GXFifo.o entries in obj_files.mk, which is important for address/order-sensitive decompilation.
Renaming was propagated through both the assembly call sites and include/functions.h, avoiding a mismatch where headers know a symbol but assembly still calls the old func_ address name.
The PR separated module organization from full C decompilation: large assembly blocks were moved into more semantically named files first, making later function-by-function matching easier.
Known SDK-style names were applied where there was strong evidence from Dolphin GX naming patterns, while many unresolved routines in GX.s remained as func_8033xxxx rather than being overnamed.
decomp_lessons: For large Dolphin library modules, splitting assembly into SDK-like source files can be useful before C conversion; it improves locality and makes future matching work easier to search.
When splitting an address-sensitive assembly object, update obj_files.mk in the intended link order at the same time as moving code.
Symbol renames should be global: update the defining .global label, all bl references, and include/functions.h together.
It is acceptable to leave uncertain routines as func_8033xxxx after a split; only promote names where the SDK/library identity is clear.
Small one-line changes in unrelated assembly files after a library rename are often call-target/comment-label propagation, not behavioral edits.
naming_conventions: Public GX API names use the GX prefix, e.g. GXInitFifoBase, GXInitFifoPtrs, GXInitFifoLimits, GXSetCPUFifo, GXSetGPFifo, GXSetVtxDesc, GXSetVtxAttrFmt, GXSetArray, GXSetMisc, GXSetDrawDone, GXWaitDrawDone.

Internal GX helper names use a double-underscore prefix, e.g. __GXFifoInit, __GXFifoReadEnable, __GXFifoReadDisable, __GXFifoLink, __GXWriteFifoIntEnable, __GXWriteFifoIntReset, __GXXfVtxSpecs, __GXSetVCD, __GXSetVAT, __GXSetDirtyState, __GXPEInit.

Pixel engine poke routines follow the GXPoke* pattern, including GXPokeAlphaMode, GXPokeAlphaRead, GXPokeAlphaUpdate, GXPokeBlendMode, GXPokeColorUpdate, GXPokeDstAlpha, GXPokeDither, and GXPokeZMode.
Unidentified local routines retained address-derived names such as func_8033D0DC and lbl_8033D070, preserving uncertainty rather than inventing names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Large code movement was done as assembly-file splitting, not source rewriting; this keeps instruction bytes stable while improving module boundaries.
The new files keep the standard assembly structure with .include "macros.inc" and .section .text, consistent with existing asm organization.
Call instructions were updated to named targets, e.g. bl __GXFifoInit, bl GXInitFifoBase, bl GXSetCPUFifo, bl GXInitFifoLimits, bl GXInitFifoPtrs, and bl __GXFifoLink.
The diff shows GX hardware FIFO/register writes using 0xCC008000 and dirty-state handling through lbl_804D5BA8; future C matching of these routines should preserve volatile/MMIO behavior carefully.
The object split likely depends on obj_files.mk ordering to keep the final link layout stable.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes are available; conclusions are based on the title, changed files, and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert asm/dolphin/gx/GXAttr.s or GXMisc.s to C.
Find PRs that replace remaining func_8033Dxxx labels in asm/dolphin/gx/GX.s with SDK names.
Search for follow-up changes to GX struct definitions around lbl_804D5BA8 and FIFO state fields.
Check later build-system PRs for additional Dolphin GX object ordering changes.
Compare this split with known Dolphin SDK GX source-file boundaries.
